metadata.title:
BOARD WILL STOP


WORK ON STREETS


IF ORDERED TO DO SO

pages.raw_text:
And still the work of the Wash.
ington Water Power company lay.
ing its tracks over the Howard
street bridges keep on regardless
of the fact that the city council
passed a resolution to stop the
work.


Corporation Counsel J. Nj. Ger
agility advised the board of public
works late Saturday not to inter-
fere. Had he advised them to inter
fere the work would have been
stopped this morning. said a mem
Der of the board today.


Mayor Daggett will not tell the
board of public works to stop tho
work. IL he tells them to stop it,
the work will be stopped, say the
board.


The city council has not told
them to stop the work, they say.
II the council directs them to do
SO, the work will be stopped


For some reason the resolution
which, 4as passed by the council
directs nothing in the minds of the
city attorneys or the mayor. II
1t does direct anything, they think
the council had no authority to so
direct.


Why does not the council or some
OF the men who passed this reso.
luton take up the work of stopping
the power company from continu.
ing on work which the council has
forbidden done?


One word from the mayor, cor
poration counsel or city council is
enough. l.. B. Handley, one of the
members of the board of public
works, stands waiting for the or
der to come. He will stop the
work In five minutes If only he is
given authority. Said he: 81 have
not received instructions to stop
this work, but stand ready at any
time the council, mayor or corpora
ton counsel SO direct. I do not
know what to make of It. was
opposed to the granting of the
franchise In the first place. Give
me the order and I will stop this
work at once. The council got
themselves into the mess and now
can't get out of it."


J. T. ONO, who is also a mem.
Der OF the board Of public works,
